_comment: calibr default config. DO NOT EDIT: create ./config.json with your overrides and run '.\calibr.ps1 init' to populate hardware detection.
_comment_exe: Absolute path to the llama-server binary ('llama-server.exe' on Windows, 'llama-server' on Linux; a CUDA build is recommended on NVIDIA). If null, the tool tries to auto-detect it on PATH and in sibling folders of scan_paths.

llama_cpp._comment_preferred_build: Optional llama.cpp release tag used by auto-fetch when no build is typed for the current run. Format: bNNNN. If a guided download uses an explicit build, calibr saves it here.

_comment_scan: List of absolute paths to recursively scan for .gguf files. Example: Windows ['C:/models', 'D:/llm'], Linux ['/mnt/models', '/home/you/models']. Configured via 'init' or manually.
_comment_dense: Model names (exact, case-sensitive) that the MoE-detection regex (`A\d+B`, `MoE`, `Mixtral`) matches but are actually dense. Add models here to force them to a context/offload plan sweep instead of the wasted MoE `--n-cpu-moe` sweep. Example: ["something-A100B-special"].

hardware._comment_budget: Fraction of VRAM considered safe from WDDM paging (Windows-specific). Empirical: 0.95 on a desktop with browser open.

bench.prompt: Write a Python function that computes the Fibonacci sequence recursively with memoization. Include an explanatory docstring and a usage example.
bench._comment_warmup: Run one small completion with identical prompt and cache_prompt=true before the real bench, to compile CUDA graphs for all batch sizes in use. Without this the first run has a 10-20s compile penalty that pollutes prompt_per_second.
bench._comment_runs: Number of times each configuration is benched. The top-level result records the median for varying metrics (prompt_tps, eval_tps, vram_peak_mib, shared_peak_mib, wddm_vram_saturation); raw per-run values live in a `runs` array. Set to 1 to disable variance reduction (the pre-v1.1.0 behavior). The CLI flag -Runs N overrides this for a single invocation.

preferences._comment_vram_usage_warning_pct: Warn in the report when baseline VRAM already used by OS/apps before a config run is at or above this percentage of total VRAM.

_comment_base: Flags applied to every benchmark. --flash-attn auto is safer than 'on' (falls back on builds that don't support it). Thread counts are injected dynamically from hardware.cpu_cores_*.

planning._comment_overhead: Approximate fixed VRAM overhead beyond model weights: compute buffers (~500), recurrent/SSM state (~50-200), mmproj (~900 if present), graph+misc (~100). Used to decide which sweep a model gets: context (fits fully) vs offload (too big).

_comment_candidates: Context / KV-cache-quantization combos to try for models that fully fit the GPU (the context sweep). q8_0 -> near-lossless, q4_0 -> unlocks ~2x more ctx at minor quality hit.
_comment_cap: Global sanity ceiling for the context sweep. Any context_candidates entry with ctx above this value is silently dropped at plan time. Set to 0 to disable. Today's highest sample-set model (Qwen3.5/3.6) caps at 262144, so this is a no-op for the curated samples but guards against accidentally pushing huge ctx values that no current GGUF supports.

wddm_detection._comment_sat: If peak_vram / total_vram exceeds this, config is flagged as 'WDDM risk'.
wddm_detection._comment_drop: Within a model, if eval_tps < drop_threshold * max_eval_tps_for_model, flag as 'WDDM paging suspected'.
wddm_detection._comment_counter: Poll Windows Perf Counter '\GPU Adapter Memory(*)\Shared Usage' during bench. Only a delta (above baseline) greater than shared_delta_confirm_mib is considered a confirmed WDDM paging event -- smaller drifts are background noise from other GPU consumers (browser, Discord).
